question_text:
Sliding Window Median

The median is the middle value in an ordered integer list. If the size of the list is even, there is no middle value, and the median is the mean of the two middle values.

Given an array nums, there is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position, return the median array for each window.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= k <= nums.length <= 105
  • -231 <= nums[i] <= 231 - 1
input_format: An array of integers nums and an integer k (window size).
output_format: An array of medians for each sliding window position.
explanation:
Two Heaps with Lazy Deletion

Use a max-heap (lo) and min-heap (hi), similar to MedianFinder. For the sliding window, implement lazy deletion — track elements scheduled for removal in a counter map. When moving the window, add the incoming element and lazily delete the outgoing element by decrementing its count and cleaning up only when it reaches the heap top.

Time: O(N log K). Space: O(K).
